title: Tetramicra bicolor
taxonomy: Tetramicra bicolor is classified as a species within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This family consists of approximately 30,000 species of flowering plants, making it one of the largest and most diverse plant families worldwide. Within the Orchidaceae family, Tetramicra bicolor belongs to the subfamily Catasetoideae. Further classification places Tetramicra bicolor in the tribe Cataseteae, which is a group of orchids known for their unique inflorescences and pollination mechanisms. This specific classification helps to identify and differentiate Tetramicra bicolor from other orchid species.

The plant's roots and leaves are rich in bioactive compounds that may possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
